svg.title: Valkyrja exception hierarchy — Go
svg.desc: Go-specific error hierarchy for Valkyrja. Three branches maintain parity with other ports. ValkyrjaThrowable is an unexported interface. ValkyrjaRuntimeException and ValkyrjaInvalidArgumentException are structs. Abstract enforcement via unexported embedded fields.
svg.text: Three branches maintain cross-port parity. Interface for Throwable · structs for Runtime and InvalidArgument.
shared across Http · Cli · Queue etc.
exported struct · one per throw site or group
ValkyrjaThrowable is an unexported interface — callers can satisfy it but cannot name or instantiate it directly from outside the package.
ComponentRuntimeException embeds an unexported field (e.g. _ struct{}). Outside packages cannot construct it as a literal — must use provided constructors.
Callers use errors.As(err, &target) to match by type across the hierarchy. errors.Is for sentinel value equality. No try/catch.
Go's (T, error) return is idiomatic. Valkyrja Go functions return (T, error) — same parity intent as TypeScript's opt-in Result layer.
Component-level categoricals use lowercase first letter (unexported). Specific concrete errors use uppercase (exported). Consistent with Go idiom.
